allow: PowerShell($pids = Get-NetTCPConnection -LocalPort 5173,5174 -State Listen -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue | Select-Object -ExpandProperty OwningProcess -Unique; if \($pids\) { $pids | ForEach-Object { Write-Output "Killing PID $_"; Stop-Process -Id $_ -Force -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue } } else { Write-Output "No listeners on 5173/5174" })

PowerShell($pids = Get-NetTCPConnection -LocalPort 5173 -State Listen -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue | Select-Object -ExpandProperty OwningProcess -Unique; if \($pids\) { $pids | ForEach-Object { Stop-Process -Id $_ -Force -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue }; "cleared $\($pids -join ','\)" } else { "nothing on 5173" })

hooks.command: rules=$(cat CLAUDE.md 2>/dev/null | head -300); printf '{"hookSpecificOutput":{"hookEventName":"SessionStart","additionalContext":"%s"}}' "$(echo "$rules" | python3 -c "import sys,json; print(json.dumps(sys.stdin.read())[1:-1])" 2>/dev/null || echo 'CLAUDE.md — see full rules in file')"

'{"hookSpecificOutput":{"hookEventName":"SessionStart","additionalContext":"STANDING RULES (adomasgaudi repos only): authoritative branch is opus-4.8 - commit + push your verified work there (build + tests pass first); the old claude/strength-training-dashboard-SdAlT canonical is DEPRECATED. Versions: bump ONLY the 3rd digit (patch), whole numbers, NEVER a 4th digit and NEVER minor/major. Assign the task code + version LAST (just before commit) and RE-derive them after any rebase so two AIs never collide. TOKEN EFFICIENCY: if the owner is spending tokens ineffectively (an expensive model on trivial work, a very long chat, repeated re-work/churn, big unverifiable batches), STOP and pop an AskUserQuestion flagging it with concrete cheaper options - the owner cannot see token waste, so silence = complicity. Full rules: CLAUDE.md HARD RULES. Command #remember X = persist X as a durable rule: one line in CLAUDE.md, or a hook in .claude/settings.json if it must run automatically; then commit+push. Command #careful X = split X into small parts, list every connected piece, show the numbered plan FIRST, then do ONE part per turn (not all at once, expect several prompts); back up or confirm before anything hard to undo."}}'

'{"hookSpecificOutput":{"hookEventName":"UserPromptSubmit","additionalContext":"PREFER POPUP QUESTIONS: The user rarely reads plain text. Whenever you would ask a question, request clarification, offer options, or seek a decision, you MUST use the AskUserQuestion tool (the multiple-choice popup) instead of asking in prose. Put the real choices as the options. Only ask in plain text if a popup genuinely cannot represent the question. TOKEN-EFFICIENCY WATCHDOG: if this prompt suggests inefficient token use (an expensive model doing trivial work, something a cheaper model or a fresh chat would handle better, or churny re-work), proactively pop an AskUserQuestion to flag it with concrete cheaper options - do not silently burn tokens."}}'

hooks.statusMessage: Re-anchoring on the locked task (if any)

hooks.command: $s = git status --porcelain; if ($s) { '{"systemMessage":"Uncommitted changes - when this work is ready, ask me to commit it and bump the version (keep the on-screen version in lockstep, per CLAUDE.md)."}' }

hooks.statusMessage: Checking HARD RULES (model stamp / version lockstep)
